Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "GreenStack", "Derek Hollis", "Priya Venkatesh", or "Noah Fitzgerald" (or similar) to find GreenStack relocation details and account communications. Expected findings: Derek's original relocation requests, Priya Venkatesh's completed move with special lab equipment (Keysight oscilloscope, PCB testing rig, electronics surcharge $650), Noah Fitzgerald's move to Seattle (apartment at 412 Boylston Ave E Apt 3B, flight Alaska AS 422 April 14), and the April bulk relocations request from Derek (Mei-Lin Zhao hazmat batteries, Joaquín Reyes solar rig).
OE 2: Search CRM for GreenStack account and deal status using crm_search_deals or crm_search_companies with queries related to "GreenStack" (or similar). Expected findings: GreenStack Energy as Active client (since May 2025), Emeka as AM, deal_greenstack_q2 ($13,500 for Venkatesh + Fitzgerald, stage: contractsent), deal_greenstack_apr2026_zhao_reyes ($9,440 for Zhao + Reyes, stage: inprogress), and CRM engagement notes on the April relocation wave.
OE 3: Search Airtable for GreenStack relocation records using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records with queries related to "GreenStack" (or similar). Expected findings: Priya Venkatesh (Houston→Seattle, Completed, Fatimah coordinator, Swift Relocations, electronics surcharge $650), Noah Fitzgerald (Philadelphia→Seattle, Completed, Fatimah coordinator), Tomás Herrera (Chicago→Austin, In Progress, Fatimah coordinator), Mei-Lin Zhao (Houston→Portland, In Progress, hazmat Class 9 lithium batteries, Fatimah coordinator), Joaquín Reyes (Phoenix→Seattle, In Progress, 900-lb solar rig, Javier Morales coordinator).
OE 4: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Mosaic", "Tamara Byrd", "prototype", "damage", or "incident" (or similar) to find Mosaic account communications. Expected findings: Tamara Byrd's original relocation requests, Zara Okoye prototype damage report ($90K value, cracked servo motor housing, misaligned joint calibration), Emeka's formal incident report to Tamara and Rafael Mendes, the insurance coverage gap discussion ($50K vendor liability cap = $40K gap), and no follow-up from Tamara after the incident report.
OE 5: Search CRM for Mosaic account and deal status using crm_search_deals or crm_search_companies. Expected findings: Mosaic Robotics as newest client (since Jan 2026, CRM type: Client; Airtable Client Accounts shows status: Onboarding), deal_mosaic_q2 ($4,500 Andre Washington, closedwon), deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk flagged "RISK: DAMAGE CLAIM", plus deals for Hartley and Tanaka in progress. CRM engagement logging the damage incident with chain-of-custody timeline, Blessing Okafor's remote sign-off, and insurance gap analysis.
OE 6: Search Airtable for Mosaic relocation records. Expected findings: Andre Washington (Completed), Zara Okoye (Savannah→Atlanta, Completed, prototype damage), Deshawn Hartley (Tampa→Denver, In Progress, hard April 19 deadline for Automate 2026 trade show), Keiko Tanaka (Columbus→Seattle, In Progress, 400-lb laser cutter, DOT hazmat placarding required).
OE 7: Search Linear for Mosaic and GreenStack related tickets using linear_list_issues or linear_list_projects. Expected findings: GreenStack relocation tracking (issues #313-#318, with Fitzgerald tickets #316-#318 showing In Progress/Todo despite Airtable showing Completed), Mosaic policy config bug (P1 fix for malformed JSON - false approvals: $1,200 standing desk over $800 cap, $350 coworking excluded category), DOT compliance audit project (Keiko Tanaka missing signed placarding certificate), stipend duplicate detection ticket (Andre Washington and Kevin Tran identical $89 receipts from TechComfort Supply).
OE 8: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages for internal discussions about GreenStack and Mosaic. Expected findings: prototype damage escalation in #operations, insurance gap analysis from Marcus ($40K exposure), Blessing Okafor's admission about packing sign-off without physical verification while juggling concurrent moves, ExpenseBot false approval discussions, DOT compliance audit updates, coordinator capacity assignments, Emeka's original handoff messages for both accounts.
OE 9: Search emails using search_emails with query "Noah" or "Fitzpatrick" (or similar) to investigate how Noah Fitzgerald's (GreenStack) relocation "landed" and cross-reference with Airtable and Slack findings from OEs 3 and 8. Expected discovery: Fatimah Al-Rashidi confused Noah Fitzgerald (GreenStack, Marketing Manager, Philadelphia→Seattle) with Noah Fitzpatrick (Axiom Precision Manufacturing, Senior Engineer, Philadelphia→Seattle) during concurrent April relocations. She searched "Noah," pulled the wrong record, and sent GreenStack's confirmed apartment (412 Boylston Ave E, Apt 3B, Capitol Hill, $2,100/mo) and flight (Alaska AS 422, April 14, PHL→SEA) to the Axiom employee. The error is discoverable primarily through email confirmations (the same apartment and flight details appear in emails sent to both Noahs) and Slack messages in #operations (where Fatimah confirmed the Axiom/Fitzpatrick intake using GreenStack's details). Note: despite Slack and email references to a Linear ticket and CRM entry for Axiom/Fitzpatrick, no corresponding Linear ticket or CRM deal exists in the database — the mix-up is undetected and must be discovered by comparing confirmation details across the two relocations.
OE 10: Look up Tamara Byrd's contact details using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts. Expected finding: tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com.
OE 11: Send check-in email (via send_email) from emeka.diallo@moveops.com to tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com addressing Tamara's concerns: the prototype damage incident (Zara Okoye, cracked servo motor, $90K value, $40K insurance gap), what MoveOps has done in response (formal incident report, insurance claim, replacement shipping offer at MoveOps' cost, chain-of-custody timeline), and current status of active Mosaic relocations.
OE 12: Create CRM engagement (via crm_create_engagement) for GreenStack Energy logging findings: Priya Venkatesh relocation completed successfully ($7,289, highest-margin move), Noah Fitzgerald relocation status and any issues discovered (potential mix-up with Axiom's Noah Fitzpatrick), active April relocations (Zhao hazmat, Reyes oversize), and expansion readiness assessment.
OE 13: Create CRM engagement (via crm_create_engagement) for Mosaic Robotics logging findings: prototype damage claim ($90K, $40K insurance gap), Blessing Okafor root cause, DOT compliance gap (Keiko Tanaka missing certificate), ExpenseBot false approvals, stipend fraud (Andre Washington duplicate receipt), Deshawn Hartley trade show deadline, and Tamara Byrd's non-response.
OE 14: Create Linear issue (via linear_create_issue) for any unresolved operational items not already being tracked - e.g., Noah Fitzgerald/Fitzpatrick mix-up correction, Mosaic Tamara Byrd follow-up coordination, or GreenStack expansion preparation.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
